releases.author: LJrobinson
releases.description:
Fixes the final remaining automated Obsidian review warning.

Changes:
- Updates getDailyActivityBucketStates to return a typed boolean[].
- Replaces new Array(...) with new Array<boolean>(...) to avoid inferred any[].
- Keeps behavior unchanged.

Validation:
- npm run build passes.
- No direct .style. assignments introduced.

Notes:
- No user-facing feature changes.

releases.author: LJrobinson
releases.description:
Maintenance release for LJ OS.

Changes:
- Reduces automated Obsidian review warnings around render/data typing.
- Adds typed DOM helper wrappers around activeDocument usage.
- Adds Git Wall snapshot/rendering types.
- Adds safe coercion helpers for strings, numbers, arrays, dates, and Git error values.
- Tightens Git command result/error handling.
- Regenerates build output.

Validation:
- npm run build passes.
- manifest.json and versions.json validate as JSON.
- Plugin smoke tested locally in Obsidian.

No user-facing feature changes.

releases.author: LJrobinson
releases.description:
## Summary

Adds the Daily Activity Bar as a movable Git Wall dashboard section.

## Added

- Daily Activity Bar with 7 time buckets
- Optional moon phase icon
- Optional Open-Meteo weather icon using manual latitude/longitude
- Weather context persistence in daily cached JSON
- Optional 3-day and 7-day cached Activity views
- Mutually exclusive 3-day/7-day settings
- Dashboard Layout integration
- README documentation

## Review cleanup

- Manifest name/version aligned for release assets
- Removed home-directory expansion to avoid system identity/path reads
- Added minimal build script for verification: `node --check main.js`

## Notes

The Activity Bar works without weather, location, API access, or logins. Weather and moon icons are optional ambient context only. Range views read cached daily JSON and do not rescan repositories.

## Verification

- `node --check .\main.js` passed
- `npm run build` passed
- Manual Obsidian plugin reinstall verified default layout/order
releases.name: 1.1.0 — Daily Activity Bar

releases.author: LJrobinson
releases.description:
## LJ os 1.0.2

Obsidian community review cleanup release.

### Changed

- Removed local machine identity metadata from generated Git activity snapshots.
- Removed hostname-based machine field from plugin output.
- Renamed plugin display name to `LJ os`.
- Added npm lockfile for reproducible dependency resolution.
- Added GitHub release asset artifact attestation workflow.

### Notes

This release keeps the plugin local-first and focused only on necessary local Git activity scanning. No logins, APIs, telemetry, cloud services, or system identity tracking.
releases.name: LJ os 1.0.2

releases.author: LJrobinson
releases.description:
## LJ OS 1.0.0

Initial stable release of LJ OS for Obsidian.

LJ OS is a local-first Git Wall dashboard for Obsidian desktop. It scans user-configured local Git repositories, stores cached activity JSON inside the vault, and generates daily Git Wall output without logins, APIs, cloud services, or a companion app.

### Highlights

- Standalone Obsidian plugin
- No login, API keys, cloud service, analytics, or companion app
- Local Git metadata only
- Scan roots and tracked repositories workflow
- Discover repositories from local folders/drives
- Startup and interval background scans
- Cached Git Wall / Daily Note rendering
- Dashboard section ordering
- Customizable labels
- Desktop-only by design

### Release assets

This release includes:

- `main.js`
- `manifest.json`
releases.name: 1.0.0 - Standalone local Git Wall dashboard for Obsidian

releases.author: LJrobinson
releases.description:
## What's new

This release focuses on making Git Wall easier to understand at a glance.

### Documentation polish

- Expanded the README with a full Example Output Gallery
- Added examples for supported Git Wall layouts/views
- Added clearer sample JSON input documentation
- Improved local-first/privacy positioning
- Removed stale maintainer-only release checklist references
- Cleaned up GitHub markdown formatting so the repo presents better publicly

### Why it matters

Obsidian users can now see what Git Wall actually renders before installing or configuring it.

The plugin behavior is unchanged; this release is presentation/documentation polish only.
releases.name: v0.5.0 — Git Wall output gallery

releases.author: LJrobinson
releases.description:
## v0.4.0 — Git Wall settings polish

This release makes Git Wall easier to configure, easier to understand, and cleaner to use inside Obsidian.

### Added

- Customizable Git Wall labels and section titles
- Emoji on/off rendering
- Section visibility toggles
  - Summary section
  - Repository section
  - Tidy-up section
- Summary styles
  - Callout
  - Scoreboard
  - Pit Wall
- Repository views
  - Table
  - Status Cards
- Table formats
  - Compact
  - Standard
  - Detailed
  - Emoji Board
- Tidy-up views
  - Queue
  - Shutdown Checklist
- Conditional settings UI
  - Summary style only appears when the summary section is enabled
  - Repo view only appears when the repository section is enabled
  - Table format only appears when Repo view is set to Table
  - Tidy view only appears when the tidy-up section is enabled
- “Customize Labels” section with a reset labels button

### Changed

- Rebranded the rendered Daily Note section around **Git Wall**
- Improved settings layout with clearer groups:
  - Paths
  - Display
  - Customize Labels
- Clarified that JSON is internal CLI-to-plugin data
- Removed the raw JSON open command
- Release assets are now only:
  - `main.js`
  - `manifest.json`

### Notes

Git Wall is designed to work with the companion CLI:

https://github.com/LJrobinson/obsidian-lj-os-cli

The CLI generates daily local Git activity JSON. This plugin reads that JSON and renders it into a customizable Obsidian Daily Note section.

The plugin does not call Git directly, use the GitHub API, access OS telemetry, open external apps, or send data anywhere.

### Manual install

Copy these files into:

```text
<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/lj-os/
```

Required files:
```text
main.js
manifest.json
```

Then reload community plugins in Obsidian and enable LJ OS / Git Wall.
releases.name: v0.4.0 — Git Wall settings polish
